untranslatable_reason: BARR pattern requires visual/morphological analysis of price formation (mountain range shape, foothills, rounding top) that cannot be expressed with standard technical indicators. The pattern depends on identifying specific candle sequence shapes and relative height relationships across a multi-bar formation, which exceeds the capability of point-in-time indicator values. Conditions like 'prices round over at the top', 'mountains rise well above foothills', and 'sharp decline after mountains end' require pattern recognition beyond allowed indicators.
translation_notes: Attempted to capture core SHORT signal elements: elevated volume at start (hvol_20), price above rising trend (sma_20), and upside momentum (higher highs). Exit on trend reversal (close crosses above sma_20). However, this is an imperfect proxy—the true BARR pattern requires candlestick formation analysis and relative peak/trough geometry that standard technical indicators cannot replicate. A proper implementation would need custom pattern recognition logic or explicit bar-by-bar morphology detection.
